name: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez
subject: Hey, Peter, it's Alexandria!
body:
[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/18910?refcode=em20241227-1165-foote&t=1001&akid=1165%2E342411%2E5C__Zo ]Squad Victory Fund

Hey, Peter, it's
Alexandria!

I wanted to reach out personally today to talk about something very near
and dear to my heart: our Squad.

When I first teamed up with my incredible sisters in service — Ilhan,
Ayanna, and Rashida — it was clear that we were building more than just a
social network. We are a movement. Together, we’ve fought tooth and nail
for the progressive values that matter most to the communities we
represent.

Of course, we’ve faced big challenges. We’ve been up against the most
powerful corporate interests in the world, seeking to buy their way into
our politics and silence the voices of everyday people. But we’re still
here. Time and again, we’ve demonstrated that with your support, our Squad
is a resilient force. No matter what obstacles we face, our commitment to
fighting for a future that benefits us all remains unwavering.

Now, we’re up against an important deadline, and we’ve set a goal to raise
$50,000 by midnight on December 31 to power our movement into the new
year. So I wanted to personally ask you to help us hit our goal.

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/19213?c0=svfem-20241227-movement.qPxkT-&refcode=em20241227-1165&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1002&akid=1165%2E342411%2E5C__Zo ]Will you chip in $5 or more today to ensure our strategy to push back
against Trump and his administration is strong, clear, and effective?

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/19213?c0=svfem-20241227-movement.qPxkT-&refcode=em20241227-1165&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1021&akid=1165%2E342411%2E5C__Zo ]Contribute ››

Thank you for standing with our movement.

Pa’lante,

Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez
Squad Victory Fund

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe:

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://act.squadvictoryfund.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=1165%2E342411%2E5C__Zo

Paid for by The Squad Victory Fund, a joint fundraising committee authorized by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, Rashida Tlaib for Congress, Courage to Change, Inspiring Leadership Has a Name PAC, Power of Us PAC, Rooted in Community Leadership PAC, Cori Bush for Congress, and Bowman for Congress.

In addition to what’s legally required, The Squad Victory Fund does not accept contributions from: (1) corporations, associations, or their PACs; (2) individuals registered as federal lobbyists or under FARA; (3) from the PACs, lobbyists, or SEC-named executives of fossil fuel companies; or (4) from the Fraternal Order of Police.

Contributions or gifts to The Squad Victory Fund are not tax deductible.

Contributions to The Squad Victory Fund will be allocated as follows: 8.33% to each of Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, Rashida Tlaib for Congress, Courage to Change, Inspiring Leadership Has a Name PAC, Power of Us PAC, Rooted in Community Leadership PAC, Cori Bush for Congress, Politivist PAC, Jamaal Bowman for Congress, and For the Children PAC.

Any contributor may designate their contribution for a particular participant. The allocation formula above may change if any contributor makes a contribution that, when allocated, would exceed the amount that the contributor may lawfully give to any participant.

After contributing to The Squad Victory Fund, you will automatically be signed up to receive email updates from Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, Rashida Tlaib for Congress, Cori Bush for Congress, and Jamaal Bowman for Congress. You may of course opt-out by unsubscribing at any time.

To contribute via check, please address to The Squad Victory Fund c/o MBA Consulting Group, PO Box 15845, Washington D.C., 20003

Email us: info@squadvictoryfund.com

name: Jeremiah Regan
subject: Theology 101
body:
_**A message from our sponsor...**_ The study of God [](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=d1b06c558a404db1a32cb6d52abe0691&_e=3KujfwASBm-A3cBsmTBcrgF9FKoBKiL9FrC9TxCgkRQhscZVK_wfWUnAvgmjD8H5LQKf7a8CeziyYiAxT0_BEHCeOBvRkQfB6mZZnNvi2y057kbL0sWH3pDjwnBtMec-xOQaVnyp2E7nFWGB-3MAJ6z4enHWvkTglWSHE8NCzmvlnW6qA9PoasjpYTA2QTOYVlikQPfNak-RmYLw9F6luMCWOEQeB0PEJjz2Fl9Ytp9n4yM-yGo6aBOOEH52YXKjEc-j9A_6PwjxawWtFc3QnBQdzF7NRVeO1mA_tDuKAtTaul9FW7RMLn3TdP7GZK3mq-yw_g1n6GoFgLfeJlYN91CDNkKmAnYGuw3CUByZVLdahuKGw_2YQL4dIdtHdVv2Z3rRUSD3d1FqdYfSg5jMK1MQ15dpWFxzyaShoAn2H27iVdoNEi0WRexYHY-IQXlSCa9tsgZ-0b3KC1mzNtHKLA%3D%3D) Dear Fellow American, I hope your day is going well. I’m writing to let you know about something I think you’ll find useful in your learning. Reason and speech are gifts from God that enable us to think about God and to worship Him. [](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=d1b06c558a404db1a32cb6d52abe0691&_e=3KujfwASBm-A3cBsmTBcrgF9FKoBKiL9FrC9TxCgkRQhscZVK_wfWUnAvgmjD8H5LQKf7a8CeziyYiAxT0_BEHCeOBvRkQfB6mZZnNvi2y057kbL0sWH3pDjwnBtMec-xOQaVnyp2E7nFWGB-3MAJ6z4enHWvkTglWSHE8NCzmvlnW6qA9PoasjpYTA2QTOYVlikQPfNak-RmYLw9F6luMCWOEQeB0PEJjz2Fl9Ytp9n4yM-yGo6aBOOEH52YXKjEc-j9A_6PwjxawWtFc3QnBQdzF7NRVeO1mA_tDuKAtQFyJOzqB16KSM7XOeSiXR3ZEKyX32FB9Kgli_YfC9ACThQrbgwk-w-UEHbwCEz7DcsPbeoUuZQchUakvkKKAZgQlfC4y07WaQE3fqdQN7GMhUNQ0Mblx4JL8NxPre_aRsT1_obyAwcrpA1mih2j5N5pIKFzvJP1cvqKEBoQ5NjxQ%3D%3D) One of the chief aims of education at Hillsdale College is to understand what kind of being we are, as well as our purpose. That, in turn, makes it possible to begin to understand our relationship to God.

The study of God, also known as “theology,” has had a profound impact on Western civilization, stretching back thousands of years to the ancient Hebrews. And that’s what Hillsdale’s **“Theology 101”** online course is about—the development of theology from the Old Testament through the twentieth century.

**“Theology 101”** has been a popular course, so I’m pleased to let you know that it is [**now available for you to own on DVD**](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=d1b06c558a404db1a32cb6d52abe0691&_e=3KujfwASBm-A3cBsmTBcrgF9FKoBKiL9FrC9TxCgkRQhscZVK_wfWUnAvgmjD8H5LQKf7a8CeziyYiAxT0_BEHCeOBvRkQfB6mZZnNvi2y057kbL0sWH3pDjwnBtMec-xOQaVnyp2E7nFWGB-3MAJ6z4enHWvkTglWSHE8NCzmvlnW6qA9PoasjpYTA2QTOYVlikQPfNak-RmYLw9F6luMCWOEQeB0PEJjz2Fl9Ytp9n4yM-yGo6aBOOEH52YXKjEc-j9A_6PwjxawWtFc3QnBQdzF7NRVeO1mA_tDuKAtT0pd0Nxl8bH9mDfTkr9tFuP2cTqvjaHwpOvOUkS3AnK_O7mnex7GjeE11lyuH8JVFtLeEl7mX4lAYdKloZ6YXFOtlyXWapZtfn1IfrfEDcbLJlyXzMCakcnRH9Opr_Z2d8Jj3FGAYOirl8AO1qWInTKV7W9Rf1b0J2LmwNIBvdkQ%3D%3D). You can add it to your home library or give it as a gift to a friend or relative.

We do have a limited number of sets in stock, though—and they go fast—so don’t delay in getting your copy of **“Theology 101”** on DVD. Secure yours right now at: **[https://secured.hillsdale.edu/support-hillsdales-online-courses-theology-101-dvd](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=d1b06c558a404db1a32cb6d52abe0691&_e=3KujfwASBm-A3cBsmTBcrgF9FKoBKiL9FrC9TxCgkRQhscZVK_wfWUnAvgmjD8H5LQKf7a8CeziyYiAxT0_BEHCeOBvRkQfB6mZZnNvi2y057kbL0sWH3pDjwnBtMec-xOQaVnyp2E7nFWGB-3MAJ6z4enHWvkTglWSHE8NCzmvlnW6qA9PoasjpYTA2QTOYVlikQPfNak-RmYLw9F6luMCWOEQeB0PEJjz2Fl9Ytp9n4yM-yGo6aBOOEH52YXKjEc-j9A_6PwjxawWtFc3QnBQdzF7NRVeO1mA_tDuKAtSPIp8ka00pxIzzBvCdqMbili0ZiCPKNYvpEg40M8pLLrcEsiML3ckkhWMQ-16gVWzqw7txv7sdTFFfrwWwla-eQ2vmgTidnVS1nfc2mWWZwI4i6xEmT3ynFltvXyWVPdUKl9zzdeE-MYQw2wWXB_foMZrWTzc0jnwukqH7VwP_Hw%3D%3D)** Best regards,

Jeremiah Regan, Ph.D.
Executive Director of Online Learning
Hillsdale College [Unsubscribe](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/e/encryptedUnsubscribe?_r=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_s=d1b06c558a404db1a32cb6d52abe0691&_t=wG1GDcUOb2yfPSEv3XR3lGxb0wBvx3VuqiQm-f4G1rpn0nTfsLs0FCKnFy5MpiAdHFWvc1FyEFfQRPDNb_OMZ0Wx3USjgWwFZK91O-2yNmX1b1GuVPtbJ-XVpwWTKntr)

name: Mark Takano
subject: I wanted to re-introduce myself!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Mark Takano For Congress P
body:
what pushes me >>>

| Peter, my path to Congress was not decided in smoke-filled rooms or carefully shaped by consultants in DC. **My commitment to public service was built across generations, and instilled in me by my family.**

During World War II, my parents and grandparents were sent to Japanese American internment camps because of racist, fear-mongering policies. They lost __everything__ and had no choice but to rebuild their entire lives after the war [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/99369494/519702981/238616526?amount=7&refcode=MTFC_20241227_EMDF_E1_T0_AC_RS&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDM2MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyYjIyMWU4MS05Y2M0LWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDIyNDgyYTkzYWYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=NPz5BqifEu8KoyMmIFoGsjqrmfOZtuDSH19ywtejnGE=) _Despite_ being cast aside as second-class citizens, they instilled in me a deep sense of civic responsibility and commitment to public service. To love our country _despite_ its flaws, and to fight for the more perfect union that our Constitution promises. **Despite it all, they persevered.**

It’s what drove me to become a public school teacher for 20 years and eventually run for Congress. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/99369495/519702982/238616526?amount=7&refcode=MTFC_20241227_EMDF_E1_T0_AC_RS&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDM2MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyYjIyMWU4MS05Y2M0LWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDIyNDgyYTkzYWYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=NPz5BqifEu8KoyMmIFoGsjqrmfOZtuDSH19ywtejnGE=) When I ran for Congress in 1994, bigotry would rear its ugly head when I was forcibly and **publicly outed as gay** by my Republican opponent.

Despite it all, **I persevered and was a sitting member of Congress when the Supreme Court legalized gay marriage in 2015.**

Today, we’re seeing some of that same racism, xenophobia, and homophobia again – especially from my MAGA counterparts in Congress. Attacks on immigrants, LGBTQ+ Americans, and women have been rising in recent years and threaten to derail decades of progress.

But despite it all, **we will prevail.**

[**Peter, hate is able to prevail when good people do nothing. So I’m asking for your support today – we can fight back against MAGA extremism if we band together! Will you chip in $7 today to stop MAGA’s erosion of our rights and help elect Democrats win? »**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/99369496/519702983/238616526?amount=7&refcode=MTFC_20241227_EMDF_E1_T0_AC_RS&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDM2MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyYjIyMWU4MS05Y2M0LWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDIyNDgyYTkzYWYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=NPz5BqifEu8KoyMmIFoGsjqrmfOZtuDSH19ywtejnGE=) If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | [CONTRIBUTE $7 »](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/99369502/519702989/238616526?amount=7&amounts=3,7,10&express_lane=true&refcode=MTFC_20241227_EMDF_E1_T0_AC_RS&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDM2MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyYjIyMWU4MS05Y2M0LWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDIyNDgyYTkzYWYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=NPz5BqifEu8KoyMmIFoGsjqrmfOZtuDSH19ywtejnGE=)

clean_body: what pushes me >>> | Peter, my path to Congress was not decided in smoke-filled rooms or carefully shaped by consultants in DC. My commitment to public service was built across generations, and instilled in me by my family. During World War II, my parents and grandparents were sent to Japanese American internment camps because of racist, fear-mongering policies. They lost everything and had no choice but to rebuild their entire lives after the war = Despite being cast aside as second-class citizens, they instilled in me a deep sense of civic responsibility and commitment to public service. To love our country despite its flaws, and to fight for the more perfect union that our Constitution promises. Despite it all, they persevered. It’s what drove me to become a public school teacher for 20 years and eventually run for Congress. = When I ran for Congress in 1994, bigotry would rear its ugly head when I was forcibly and publicly outed as gay by my Republican opponent. Despite it all, I persevered and was a sitting member of Congress when the Supreme Court legalized gay marriage in 2015. Today, we’re seeing some of that same racism, xenophobia, and homophobia again – especially from my MAGA counterparts in Congress. Attacks on immigrants, LGBTQ+ Americans, and women have been rising in recent years and threaten to derail decades of progress. But despite it all, we will prevail. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | = = = Sincerely, Mark Takano | | Paid for by Mark Takano For Congress P.O. name: Matt Shinners, Forward Party
subject: Looking Back, Moving Forward - Strategy Team Reflections
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Forward Party and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
What we learned and where we're going in 2025.

| | [ ](https://home.forwardparty.com/r?u=JgyyvCfQwldlSeD7KJl_Ih7269o_4hwqOhPs1_PHvgI&e=61868e7acdd36ae0ddf3e39aa8429171&utm_source=forwardparty&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=end_of_year_series_strategy&n=1)
---
Friday, December 27, 2024 | Peter -- Hi all, I’m Matt Shinners, the Chief Strategy and Operating Officer here at Forward Party. Over the next week, while we’re on break, I’ve had each member of our team write up the takeaways from their internal debrief. These debriefs involved a conversation about what went well in 2024, where we learned lessons to improve in the future, and what projects they are thinking of engaging in in 2025 to advance our goals. **Please don’t just read these debriefs—follow the link below to submit your own takeaways from 2024 and suggestions for 2025.** | [](https://home.forwardparty.com/2024_end_of_year_feedback?e=61868e7acdd36ae0ddf3e39aa8429171&utm_source=forwardparty&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=end_of_year_series_strategy&n=2 "Give Us Your Feedback!") [Give Us Your Feedback!](https://home.forwardparty.com/2024_end_of_year_feedback?e=61868e7acdd36ae0ddf3e39aa8429171&utm_source=forwardparty&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=end_of_year_series_strategy&n=2)
---
We will look at and consider all submissions as we plan out our quarterly goals for 2025. We’ll even feature 10 of our favorites as our New Years Resolutions!
I’m going to start with a review of our 2024 strategic focus.
**What Went Well**
In 2024, we started with the objective of running down-ballot candidates in key races to boost non-extremist candidates at the top of the ticket. However, as we engaged in that work, we found out that we had a lot of candidate recruitment, endorsement, and support processes to figure out.
While we looked for races that fit that profile, we didn’t limit ourselves to them. Our next email will discuss that work, but the takeaway from a strategic perspective is that we spent 2024 building out our Minimum Viable Product. We now have systems and processes in place to recruit candidates, put them through an endorsement consideration pipeline, and then support the ones we choose to endorse. That’s a huge accomplishment that we’re proud of in 2024.
**Lessons Learned**
A big lesson learned is that we need to perfect these systems and build them to scale, so that’s going to be a big focus of 2025.
We also know that we have the market share of party members who are passionate about electoral reform. If you’re someone who believes in open primaries and/or ranked-choice voting, you’ve heard of Forward, and joined if you’re going to. We need to poll our membership to find out who they are outside of their reform advocacy, and figure out who else will want to join a new party based on our values.
Finally, we need to be super targeted in all the work that we do in 2025. We used ‘24 to stress test the systems we built, and we feel good about them. As noted above, we need to iterate on them. To do so, we’ll take advantage of the smaller list of elections in the ‘25 off-cycle to really test some of our hypotheses. We need to know what the profile is of a district where Forward can take off. Is it a district where one party dominates? Where Independents outnumber party-registered voters? Where Dunkin’ is more popular than Starbucks? We don’t know, but we will by the end of the year.
[These are our thoughts from 2024, but we want to hear from you! Please head to this link and fill out your thoughts on lessons learned, the direction the party should head in, and projects we should tackle! We will read them all, and we will highlight our top 10, along with the people who submitted them, in early January as our New Years Resolutions.](https://home.forwardparty.com/2024_end_of_year_feedback?e=61868e7acdd36ae0ddf3e39aa8429171&utm_source=forwardparty&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=end_of_year_series_strategy&n=3)
**2025 Projects**
* Debrief with all states to learn what we can from their work and experiences
* Regularly poll our membership, and drive engagement in social media surveys
* Build out our target personas - the people out there who would be interested in Forward, with the right message and messenger
* Use the 2025 off-cycle elections to engage in a Proof of Concept phase to figure out the most fertile ground for party building

If you're still feeling the season of giving, consider donating to Forward this season!
[](https://home.forwardparty.com/donate_2024_eoy?e=61868e7acdd36ae0ddf3e39aa8429171&utm_source=forwardparty&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=end_of_year_series_strategy&n=4 "Chip In $25 Today!") [Chip In $25 Today!](https://home.forwardparty.com/donate_2024_eoy?e=61868e7acdd36ae0ddf3e39aa8429171&utm_source=forwardparty&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=end_of_year_series_strategy&n=4)
---
Happy Holidays,
Matt Shinners

Over the next week, while we’re on break, I’ve had each member of our team write up the takeaways from their internal debrief. These debriefs involved a conversation about what went well in 2024, where we learned lessons to improve in the future, and what projects they are thinking of engaging in in 2025 to advance our goals. Please don’t just read these debriefs—follow the link below to submit your own takeaways from 2024 and suggestions for 2025. | "Give Us Your Feedback!" We will look at and consider all submissions as we plan out our quarterly goals for 2025. We’ll even feature 10 of our favorites as our New Years Resolutions! I’m going to start with a review of our 2024 strategic focus. What Went Well In 2024, we started with the objective of running down-ballot candidates in key races to boost non-extremist candidates at the top of the ticket. However, as we engaged in that work, we found out that we had a lot of candidate recruitment, endorsement, and support processes to figure out. While we looked for races that fit that profile, we didn’t limit ourselves to them. Our next email will discuss that work, but the takeaway from a strategic perspective is that we spent 2024 building out our Minimum Viable Product. We now have systems and processes in place to recruit candidates, put them through an endorsement consideration pipeline, and then support the ones we choose to endorse. That’s a huge accomplishment that we’re proud of in 2024. We need to poll our membership to find out who they are outside of their reform advocacy, and figure out who else will want to join a new party based on our values. Finally, we need to be super targeted in all the work that we do in 2025. We used ‘24 to stress test the systems we built, and we feel good about them. As noted above, we need to iterate on them. To do so, we’ll take advantage of the smaller list of elections in the ‘25 off-cycle to really test some of our hypotheses. Where Dunkin’ is more popular than Starbucks? name: Care in Action
subject: We’re resolved to continue the fight for care 🎆
body:
Are you with us?
[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/99370221/519705515/664783965?ms=122724_nyeresolutions&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNV08vMS83NjczNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlMzY4MWRiNC1hMGM0LWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDIyNDgyYTkzYWYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KmoZ6Vy8BRU-O9LQThxvsFZ5cpaNIWUKhBe1qTxoVQY=&emci=3745b2f8-c3bc-ef11-88d0-000d3a9d5840&emdi=e3681db4-a0c4-ef11-88ce-0022482a93af&ceid=864787)
Hey Peter,
**As Republicans threaten to slash funding to Medicaid **and other critical care programs, we’re fighting back.
These cuts would be disastrous for low-income and middle-income families who rely on programs like Medicaid for general health insurance coverage, disability coverage, and healthcare for children — and we know that millions of **care workers, caregivers, and families** in our community would be affected.
So, Peter, our New Year’s resolution is to fight even harder for the care agenda in 2025 — will you resolve to do the same?
**[**_Take action right now by signing up to volunteer with Care in Action as we continue our fight for care, and contact your representatives and tell them to protect and expand health care:_**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/99370222/519705516/-901705029?ms=122724_nyeresolutions&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNV08vMS83NjczNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlMzY4MWRiNC1hMGM0LWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDIyNDgyYTkzYWYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KmoZ6Vy8BRU-O9LQThxvsFZ5cpaNIWUKhBe1qTxoVQY=&emci=3745b2f8-c3bc-ef11-88d0-000d3a9d5840&emdi=e3681db4-a0c4-ef11-88ce-0022482a93af&ceid=864787)**
**********__[Volunteer with us>>](https://click.everyaction.com/k/99370223/519705517/-901705029?ms=122724_nyeresolutions&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNV08vMS83NjczNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlMzY4MWRiNC1hMGM0LWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDIyNDgyYTkzYWYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KmoZ6Vy8BRU-O9LQThxvsFZ5cpaNIWUKhBe1qTxoVQY=&emci=3745b2f8-c3bc-ef11-88d0-000d3a9d5840&emdi=e3681db4-a0c4-ef11-88ce-0022482a93af&ceid=864787)__**********
**********__[Contact your representatives>>](https://click.everyaction.com/k/99370224/519705518/-1323163894?ms=122724_nyeresolutions&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNV08vMS83NjczNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlMzY4MWRiNC1hMGM0LWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDIyNDgyYTkzYWYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KmoZ6Vy8BRU-O9LQThxvsFZ5cpaNIWUKhBe1qTxoVQY=&emci=3745b2f8-c3bc-ef11-88d0-000d3a9d5840&emdi=e3681db4-a0c4-ef11-88ce-0022482a93af&ceid=864787)__**********
When our vital care programs are under attack, **we must stand up and fight back together** to protect the health of the most vulnerable members of our community— _and all Americans._

clean_body: Are you with us? Hey Peter, As Republicans threaten to slash funding to Medicaid and other critical care programs, we’re fighting back. These cuts would be disastrous for low-income and middle-income families who rely on programs like Medicaid for general health insurance coverage, disability coverage, and healthcare for children — and we know that millions of care workers, caregivers, and families in our community would be affected. So, Peter, our New Year’s resolution is to fight even harder for the care agenda in 2025 — will you resolve to do the same? ************** ************ When our vital care programs are under attack, we must stand up and fight back together** to protect the health of the most vulnerable members of our community— and all Americans. Thank you for taking action. Together, we can protect and expand care for everyone! Care in Action

name: Jocelyn Benson
subject: Democracy isn't just my job — it's my life's work
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JOCELYN BENSON FOR SECRETARY OF STATE
body:
OK, maybe not my first job selling tickets at the movie theater.
# [](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/5?t=1&akid=13%2E181023%2EZiX_WV)
Democracy isn't just my job -- it's my life's work. Every career choice I’ve made has been part of a lifelong mission, _my_ mission: upholding and defending American democracy.
(OK, maybe not my first job selling tickets at the movie theater. But it did teach me a lot of great skills!!)
I started as a House Page in Washington, then ended up investigating domestic terrorist cells in Alabama and tracked international extremist networks as a Marshall Scholar at Oxford. **Along the way, I learned how democracy’s opponents operate -- and what it takes to defeat them.**
That lesson stuck with me all the way from founding the Michigan Center for Election Law to serving as dean of Wayne State Law School to my amazing job today: serving as Michigan’s Secretary of State. My entire career is and was about protecting your right to vote.
**Democracy isn't just my job -- it's my life's work.**
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jb-em-20241227-1?refcode=em20241227-1&refcode2=13_181023_ZiX_WV&akid=13%2E181023%2EZiX_WV)
My backstory is a big deal because it means I don’t give up. Not when I’m getting pressure from the president and not when I’m facing attacks from billionaires on social media. Maybe it's the same stubbornness that got me through 25 marathons (including Boston at eight months pregnant!), but I believe protecting your vote is worth any challenge.
Speaking of which...looking at the challenges ahead, I’m realizing that in some way, my life’s work is just getting started. To that I say, simply, _bring it on._
Here's where you come in. Your support helps us keep Michigan's elections free, fair, and secure.
**[I’m humbly asking for your support so I can keep fighting for fair and free elections. Will you contribute to our end-of-year goal?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jb-em-20241227-1?refcode=em20241227-1&refcode2=13_181023_ZiX_WV&akid=13%2E181023%2EZiX_WV)**
One lesson from my life’s work is that democracy works best when we all jump in together — Democrats, Republicans, and Independents alike. Thanks for being part of our work to ensure every Michigander's voice rings out loud and clear.
All my best,
Jocelyn

* * *
Before you go... we need your help. Since her 2018 election, Secretary Benson has fought for free and fair elections. She’s on the front lines of defending and strengthening our democracy. **[Will you contribute today to show your support?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jb-em-20241227-1?refcode=em20241227-1&refcode2=13_181023_ZiX_WV&akid=13%2E181023%2EZiX_WV)** **[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jb-em-20241227-1?refcode=em20241227-1&refcode2=13_181023_ZiX_WV&akid=13%2E181023%2EZiX_WV)**
---

* * *
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=2&akid=13%2E181023%2EZiX_WV)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/signup/update-your-information/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe_2024/?t=3&akid=13%2E181023%2EZiX_WV)

Jocelyn Benson for Secretary of State
PO BOX 21368,
Detroit, MI 48221
PAID FOR BY JOCELYN BENSON FOR SECRETARY OF STATE

name: Team Fetterman HQ
subject: Four days left in 2024
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fetterman for PA
body:
John’s counting on yinz + youse to help him stand up for PA.
#
Hey Derek, Team Fetterman HQ here.
With just four days to go until the end of the year, our online fundraising has really slowed down. **Here’s a real screenshot of our ActBlue page so you can see what that looks like:**
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jf-email-20241227?refcode=em20241227-9732&refcode2=9732_434096_bH84Xj&akid=9732%2E434096%2EbH84Xj)
This is pretty normal after Election Day and over the holidays, but we still have fundraising goals we’ve gotta hit. And online fundraising — through emails like this one — is a pretty big part of how we do that!
**That’s why we’re aiming to bring in another 7,500 donations before the year is over. [Will you pitch in $5 or anything you can now to help us hit our goal and keep this seat 🔵?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jf-email-20241227?refcode=em20241227-9732&refcode2=9732_434096_bH84Xj&akid=9732%2E434096%2EbH84Xj)**
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jf-email-20241227?amount=5&refcode=em20241227-9732&refcode2=9732_434096_bH84Xj&akid=9732%2E434096%2EbH84Xj)**
No matter what Donald Trump does in the White House, John will do everything he can to stand up for what’s right for working folks in all 67 PA counties.
**But he can only keep it up with your support.** Trump and his buddies in the GOP spent multiple large fortunes to flip PA red this year. John’s counting on yinz + youse to help him fight back.
[**That’s why this 7,500 donation goal is so important, Derek. Will you pitch in $5 before the deadline to help John keep standing up for PA?**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jf-email-20241227?refcode=em20241227-9732&refcode2=9732_434096_bH84Xj&akid=9732%2E434096%2EbH84Xj)
Thanks,
—Team Fetterman HQ 💛

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jf-emails?refcode=em20241227-9732-footer&t=1&refcode2=9732_434096_bH84Xj&akid=9732%2E434096%2EbH84Xj)**

body:
With one click, you can become a CAVPAC Platinum Member today!

| |
---
| | |
Hello Peter,

My name is Samuel, and I'm reaching out from CAVPAC Membership Services.

I've been reviewing our membership roster, and I noticed that you're at risk of losing your priority status.

**President Trump 's former Secretary of State and CIA Director Mike Pompeo personally contacted you about joining our exclusive Platinum Membership, but we never heard back from you. I'm shocked that someone as committed as you would ignore an invitation from one of America's most respected leaders.**

That's why I'm following up today to confirm that you're not intentionally declining this offer.

I want to make sure you understand the significance of this opportunity and the impact it could have on our efforts to drive our Conservative agenda forward in 2025.

So Peter, if you're deliberately choosing not to join the Platinum Membership, I need to know so we can update our records. **[Your profile, ID: CAV123456 will be removed from our priority list, and we'll assume you're no longer interested in taking your commitment to the next level.](http://links.conservativeum.com/u/click?_t=26c2e4aa9bbe40418f61e26972138f21&_m=0014382ae1cb4e289af769798c0d8b74&_e=Sc09RY99JwpJprZuXCBQNPyJagm6SJe2h29HL-233JwGbXMJBBbd71xEdIE6WEHtNBQ0LtZ7A-ICvk_MR9ElMJnsS5_YCZIfVsGPFpujaM6_-V0IuwcDGIm85elSP0NFgsQx8X9ChltlgoFNAYpXyTFF9fFTCW273BxiovGafV8fpwkkj7PJI9b87L4O9zlKqvKuB4y5siTmJZEt09prp-vsBQ1vDZky0UXVM_wiinLsWbV0hffpF1GltfAe-4Wq0SGwYThY9hxET646HJCbiQ6DSo2RUrVqdmA-FhI0p7x7kYHRA4zlDHSjHUdr0BryEwe4djmaHqAlS8DD3gQX8cXI3y7M485xwfXQRumIIh2aswzzbDQ7VCiJw2guryX6G6ukINpvKWyamDs7Detx8G2nZBzC3zAR9VSgnJxZ2zvHDd-mjZU7tbivlHeQE11iajswWZIE29oQo0j-2qgpKu9qpaucM0gWn2K8m_qKVLb0fnpAKB-6IpG8Qa_gyehu "Your profile, ID: CAV123456 will be removed from our priority list, and we'll assume you're no longer interested in taking your commitment to the next level.")**

The holiday season is upon us, team! During this time of gratitude and reflection, we’re celebrating Democrats' incredible accomplishments over the past few years:

🏛 Confirming over 200 federal judges
🌈 Protecting same-sex and interracial marriages
♻️ Making historic investments in climate initiatives
🩺 Passing COVID-19 relief to support economic recovery
🛣️ Investing billions in infrastructure
💊 Lowering prescription drug prices
🇺🇸 Bringing manufacturing back to America

Our Democratic Senate majority delivered _huge_ results for the American people, all because of this grassroots team.

We’re organizing a card to thank President Biden, Vice President Harris, and Senate Democrats for all their hard work -- **but you haven’t signed yet, and the deadline is midnight!**

**[Sign the card to thank President Biden, Vice President Harris, and Senate Democrats!](https://go.dscc.org/go/20956?ask=5&t=8&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-ND-20241227-ThankYouCard-2024EmojiHolidayRecap-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-ND-20241227-ThankYouCard-2024EmojiHolidayRecap-Pros-sl1&akid=19261%2E9716520%2ESlgsk_)**

Comedian Dave Smith went **[SCORCHED EARTH on political hack Joe Walsh](https://substack.com/redirect/08533ad6-3f90-410b-854b-9cc698db0eb8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** for unfairly trashing Donald Trump's new Director of National Intelligence, Tulsi Gabbard, as a Russian asset.
"This is the dumbest thing I've ever heard!!" Smith snapped at Walsh, after the later had claimed Gabbard's actions have suspiciously supported the interests of Vladmir Putin.
"If I said Saddam Hussein didn't have weapons of mass destruction, that might be exactly what he wants me to say... It doesn't mean anything!!" Smith continued.
**[Watch the clash below:](https://substack.com/redirect/08533ad6-3f90-410b-854b-9cc698db0eb8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**

* * *
# Ted Cruz gets in trouble with Trump, what he does next will make your day
Donald Trump called Senator Ted Cruz to the stage during his speech at a Turning Point USA event - **[But something unexpected happened.](https://substack.com/redirect/05393fe0-df52-45e1-9e3c-ee95a45a8985?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
Cruz was nowhere to be found...
"He's in trouble" Trump said jokingly, still searching the crowd for Cruz.
**[Watch the unscripted moment unfold](https://substack.com/redirect/05393fe0-df52-45e1-9e3c-ee95a45a8985?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**

# [The Christmas Wish to End to Congestion Pricing has not Come True](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1454853&post_id=153590167&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTUzNTkwMTY3LCJpYXQiOjE3MzUzMzk3MjcsImV4cCI6MTczNzkzMTcyNywiaXNzIjoicHViLTE0NTQ4NTMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.uVBHmMSkIO7udm5pNl7G9_Svlw4GnspQ4wqUcM7iKH0)
### Bad Policies of NYC have effects on Connecticut and What Can We Learn?
| | [Jonathan Goldstein](https://substack.com/@jonathangoldstein81) and [Dr. Michael Goldstein](https://substack.com/@drmichaelgoldsteinct)
---
| Dec 27

| [](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/06f94782-3862-4601-9b7e-09d062e9f7b0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
On January 5th, New York City is set to unveil congestion pricing once again. This plan was previously stopped by court orders and a political decision by Governor Hochul until after the election. Meanwhile, MTA ridership is declining due to public safety fears, and a woman was just set on fire and killed on the subway by an illegal.
| [](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/e723016e-2c19-4539-bf5f-2798ea3109ee?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Image - NY Post
The public solution to deficits cannot be taxation or increased fares. The MTA has a public-safety issue and the system must be revitalized by making it safe and attract riders that can use the system. This death and the lengthy court case of Daniel Penny and the[ chilling effect to the Good Samaritan](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/2a401416-0fc7-40df-95f8-64d5d71c13eb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) only make this worse.
[Pledge your support](https://substack..VORDvrmK7ATpPT9dDRhyvMbSPv1R3fumcGfeOGNJhmY?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=153590167)
Thanks for reading Michael's Substack! Subscribe for free to receive new posts and support my work.
[Pledge your support](https://substack..VORDvrmK7ATpPT9dDRhyvMbSPv1R3fumcGfeOGNJhmY?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=153590167)

**What is different from the Old Proposal and How is it supposed to help?**
The new proposal has reduced rates than before (including off-peak discounts) and is set to increase with potential gridlock premiums on gridlock days.
**Where is the real congestion coming from?**
Anyone who spends any time in NYC knows that the majority of congestion is caused by:
1. Increased deliveries and double parked delivery trucks, (sometimes on both sides of the street)
2. Wonderful bike lanes that are not used for the majority of the day
3. Too many non-Taxi For Hire Vehicles operating under Lyft, Uber etc.

While New York City attempted to create this Bike Lane mecca, it has proved to be more cumbersome than intended. Bike lanes are rarely used and many ride against traffic which increases the likelihood of injury more than 5 times as riding with traffic.
**The On-Demand Lifestyle is a Major Factor of Congestion**
As a result of Covid and an on-demand society that has been perpetuated by online retailers like Amazon, Fresh-Direct, Instacart along with the use of other on-demand deliveries by Uber and other "messenger" services," congestion is at an all-time high. _The convenience has become the inconvenient truth_. Congestion fees are just another "convenience fee" that will be passed along to the consumer. City living is expensive and inflation is worse in the city as the general inflation of all goods purchased inside New York City is multiplied due to increased commercial rent and other bureaucracy.
Congestion pricing is just another cost that will be passed along to the consumer and makes New York City even more unfordable than it is. Not all workers can pass on this to their employers so many workers and independent contractors will struggle to internalize these fees of approximately $45 per week.
**Connecticut is not Immune to Congestion Pricing**
Congestion pricing is not limited to transportation and is already in energy delivery of electricity in the Public Benefits portion of your utility bill. _**Remember that most bad things that happen in New York usually come to Connecticut.**_ It is only a matter of time that toll proposals and other transit fees are proposed. In cities like Stamford, the urban sprawl without addressing the congestion is bringing the city to a standstill at rush hour. One-Lane roads to skyscrapers of Harbor Point and the the growth of residential communities of Stamford will only make this problem worse. _**The HOV lanes are an attempted solution but how many really have "commuting buddies" to carpool. It is entirely possible to see that the use of an HOV lane by a single driver will become available for a fee.**_
**What are the Alternatives to Congestion Pricing and Sticking it to the Average Consumer?**
Former NYC Mayor Bill DeBlasio started to put in place proposals to incentivize and encourage overnight deliveries back in 2019. **This was a genius idea to reduce congestion by promoting off-peak deliveries when there were fewer deliveries than there are now.** If only this was really focused, promoted and implemented this situation might have improved. Most commercial garbage is picked up at night. Why can't more action happen at night in the city that never sleeps or in many places?

In 2024, the New York City DOT offered up to $6 million in incentives to help businesses change their deliveries to off hours. A very small incentive. **This should be significantly higher coupled with incentives for consumers and businesses to change their behavior to alleviate congestion. New Yorkers receive 2 million Amazon orders per day.**
Rather than imposing, absurd, congestion, pricing, which will not increase the use of mass transit for most coming from outer boroughs and northern New Jersey and Connecticut, New York City should have exhausted alternatives to improve the lives of those that contribute to the economic system of New York. Such congestion pricing may continue to cause a further exodus from the Big Apple and encourage businesses to relocate outside of Manhattan. This could be a positive for Connecticut if managed correctly.

_**Maximizing the use of 24-hour buildings**_
As an alternative, the city should have expanded this off hours deliveries for all Amazon and other commercial and retail and consumer deliveries that can be received by Doorman on off hours. Usually these shifts are quiet and would provide additional work to be done on the night shift; it would reduce the likelihood of "napping" on the job.
While not all buildings have overnight doorman or security, most commercial buildings do and almost all commercial construction and repairs are done at night. This is a standard construction practice that is built into pricing projects without the need for "congestion pricing." Commercial construction is done at night or after 5pm as businesses that pay exorbitant rent do not want to be inconvenienced.

**Why Regulation is not the Answer?**
Any modern regulatory framework in this country is reactive and not pro-active. Congestion pricing is the latest disaster; it is another regulatory burden with no understanding of the economic impact to fund a poorly managed commuter railroad and subway system. _**This includes residents of Connecticut that earn their income in New York and are forced to commute by car as there are no great alternatives, especially in areas where there is limited to no Metro North service**_. There are also those who are afraid to use mass transit due to the increased crime and unreliability.

Connecticut is a state that typically follows the lead of some of the worst policies across the country. Connecticut residents are currently facing a plethora of environmental "Green initiatives" primarily originating out of California. In particular, the quiet yards campaigns throughout the state that mandate an immediate transition to electric backpack blowers . _**Let 's call it what it really is - Too Many Zoom calls for those fortunate to have the luxury of a work from home schedule and their inability to purchase noise cancelling headphones.**_
In addition, the Eversource conundrum continues with all of these green incentives being subsidized by retail utility payers. Up to 30 percent of residential bills are for "public benefits charges" and t[his is now to pay for their incessant marketing of these programs](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/6b33b020-e33c-46cd-80a1-6264c0c7fefa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Consumers are not to subsidize the utility company and these charges do not benefit the public. Congestion pricing definitely will not benefit the public.

**Amazon in Connecticut**
Amazon is one of Connecticut's biggest private employers with more than 17,000 employees. [It has aggressively expanded to offer same-day deliveries](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/32dd470e-9bbc-4f96-be14-31f401ec92ea?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). If not managed properly, this will only add to the congestion issues that plague our roads.
**How the Technology of NYC may be Used in Connecticut?**
So what does this all have to do with Connecticut? The technology of congestion pricing is license plate readers and EZ-Pass technology. While the roads of Connecticut are awful, especially after Covid, CT could easily put tolls on the roads to raise revenues and "reduce" congestion with no reduction. _**It is important to see the writing on the wall and write your legislators on the state and federal level to seek improvements to our roads and mass transit.**_
_**It is also time to learn from the mistakes of our New York neighbors and not repeat them which is what tends to be done here in the Constitution and soon to be the Congestion State.**_
**Thank you for your continued support. Please subscribe and spread the word.**
Dr. Michael Goldstein & Jonathan Goldstein
_The father-son team continues on_ so please spread the word and lets really rebuild CT together in a meaningful way.

name: Tom Fitton
subject: Trump Wins The Lawfare War!
body:
Happy New Year!

[INSIDE JW]

Trump Wins the Lawfare War

[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=71699&L=34168&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvdmlkZW9zL3llYXItaW4tcmV2aWV3LWVsZWN0aW9uLWludGVncml0eS12aWN0b3JpZXMtajYtc2VjcmV0cy1hbmQtbW9yZS8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=e58cc530303dcd71b5337f8b8ac85b071a027917f5c66b6d1f016c91efc9c97a]
The Left hasn’t caught on yet that Donald Trump is a fighter who
doesn’t fold easily. As our chief investigative reporter, Micah
Morrison, reports
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=71699&L=34169&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvdHJ1bXAtd2lucy1sYXdmYXJlLXdhci8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=c9e85a2635799a17f77d75ccde07b372d48f722036d93e812570560707b06efd],
Trump’s win
at the polls helped lead to wins in the courts.

> Pundits and historians will be a long time sorting out the magnitude
> of Donald Trump’s electoral victory but one thing already is
> clear: Trump not only triumphed in the presidential contest, he also
> won the lawfare war. The latter—a victory for the constitutional
> foundation of the country —may prove as consequential as the
> former.
>
> “Lawfare” is a political war fought by other means: partisan
> warfare conducted in the courts and the media. Trump spent the
> entire Biden presidency battling lawfare cases brought by
> Democrat-allied prosecutors and judges—by Justice Department
> Special Counsel Jack Smith, Manhattan District Attorney Alvin Bragg,
> Georgia District Attorney Fani Willis, New York State Attorney
> General Letitia James, New York judges Juan Merchan and Arthur
> Engoron, and others.
>
> Trump fought back in the courts and in the court of public opinion.
> His election win not only deals death blows to the Democrat-aligned
> lawfare cases, but possibly to the practice of lawfare itself.
> Let’s take a moment to survey the legal landscape:
>
> _Jack Smith Goes Down_
>
> In November 2022, President Joe Biden’s attorney general, Merrick
> Garland, appointed prosecutor Jack Smith as special counsel for two
> Justice Department investigations: the January 6, 2021, events at
> the U.S. Capitol, and separately, alleged Trump mishandling of
> classified documents. It was a particularly brazen lawfare move
> because by that time, the outline of the 2024 presidential contest
> was clear: Donald Trump was the frontrunner for the GOP presidential
> nomination and Joe Biden was signaling that he would run for
> re-election. The Biden Justice Department investigating the GOP
> presidential candidate seemed an outlandish and illegal proposition
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=71699&L=34156&F=T&&hash=eb19349dc6ab5a93d233d2cb33873deab5f3bb4efbb8ab6a8f11b5b7dbc60cb0],
> but Garland and Smith pressed on. In July, Judge Aileen Cannon had
> seen enough and dismissed
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=71699&L=34157&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly9hcG5ld3MuY29tL2FydGljbGUvdHJ1bXAtY2xhc3NpZmllZC1kb2N1bWVudHMtc21pdGgtYzY2ZDVmZmI3YmE4NmMxYjk5MWY5NWU4OWJkZWJhMGM/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=b7fff38ab9fd89c364d68ff9975d6a299d5d0f6cee246c6c455013ed42b14c55]
> the classified documents case on the grounds that the special
> counsel was unlawfully appointed. In November, after the election,
> the Justice Department threw in the towel, moving to drop all
> January 6 charges against Trump on the grounds that a sitting
> president cannot be charged with a crime. Trump rightfully claimed
> victory. “I persevered, against all odds, and WON,” he wrote on
> Truth Social. He added, “These cases, like all of the other cases
> I have been forced to go through, are empty and lawless, and should
> never have been brought,”
>
> _Bragg’s New York Criminal Case in Death Spiral _
>
> Deep blue New York produced a cadre of lawfare warriors in pursuit
> of the once and future Republican president. One of its chief
> combatants was Manhattan DA Alvin Bragg
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=71699&L=29246&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvd2hvLWlzLWFsdmluLWJyYWdnLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=676daeae04b1938e1b8df1c3b67bbdbcaa9f482aee96daafd1730eb43baf9453],
who campaigned
> for office on an anti-Trump platform, reminding voters that he had
> “sued Trump more than a hundred times.” Before charging Trump in
> April 2023 with thirty-four felony counts of falsifying business
> records—generally a low-level misdemeanor—Bragg had led a civil
> lawsuit against the Trump Foundation and criminal cases against the
> Trump Organization and its chief financial officer. Trump was
> convicted in May on the business records charges, but his lawyers
> are asking that the case be thrown out on numerous grounds,
> including that any sentencing would unconstitutionally interfere
> with Trump’s conduct of a second term in the presidency. Bragg
> recently petitioned the court to put the case on ice for the
> entirety of Trump’s second presidential term—a move the Trump
> team ridiculed as “a total failure of the prosecution” signaling
> that the case is “effectively over
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=71699&L=34170&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuZm94bmV3cy5jb20vcG9saXRpY3MvYnJhZ2ctY2FzZS1lZmZlY3RpdmVseS1vdmVyLW1ham9yLXZpY3RvcnktdHJ1bXAtb2ZmaWNpYWxzLXNheV0uIj91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=4cd18c240a46416affa5cac211a4e0370be4450b402fa585d99c23fa4d1c8382
>
> _Lawfare Judges Under Pressure_
>
> Presiding over the flurry of appeals
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=71699&L=34159&F=T&&hash=65c3597125b198c8f32e01f045e59b0d35949e5292521ba708632157f1baf62d]
> in the business-records case is Justice Juan Merchan, another New
> Yorker with a lawfare pedigree. Earlier this month, Merchan threw
> out
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=71699&L=34160&F=T&&hash=f8b56828be651a849d203aa9197ea59fd90286edc2c4f33f3ce8907252ee49ec]
> Trump’s appeal to dismiss the case on the basis of presidential
> immunity. Like most New York judges, Merchan rose through the ranks
> of the Democratic Party’s political machine, which plays a
> significant role in state judicial appointments. Before becoming a
> judge, Merchan served as a prosecutor in the Manhattan DA’s office
> and worked for the New York attorney general. In 2006, Mayor Michael
> Bloomberg appointed him to a family court judgeship, and he was
> elevated to criminal court in 2009. In July, Merchan received a
> “caution letter
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=71699&L=34161&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly94LmNvbS9Ub21GaXR0b24vc3RhdHVzLzE3OTE2Nzk1MjExMTgxNDY5Njld4oCdP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=e8ff6913b0e346e1c07b84b78112970985f7e1d7d073d15cf9e56a5a5a9118b9
from the New
> York Commission of Judicial Conduct warning him about donations to
> Joe Biden and other Democratic causes. Merchan’s daughter, Loren,
> is president of the left-wing digital advertising firm, Authentic
> Campaigns. Juan Merchan will have plenty of power over the Trump
> appeals in the coming months, but he will not have the final word.
> Trump can appeal to higher New York courts and, ultimately, the U.S.
> Supreme Court.
>
> Trump also faced a high-stakes legal assault from New York State
> Attorney General Letitia James in a civil fraud case presided over
> by Justice Arthur Engoron. James and Engoron both came up through
> the progressive ranks of the New York Democratic Party. Like Alvin
> Bragg, James used Trump as a punching bag in her campaign for
> political office. She denounced
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=71699&L=34162&F=T&&hash=ee038e192371f6ca48eaac5d0f6a06d56e91038b8b61ff8c98dda6c9bc068c7e]
> Trump as an “illegitimate president” and vowed to “shine a
> bright light into every corner of his real estate dealings.”
> Engoron, a longtime Democrat, protested the Vietnam War at Columbia
> University and has been a member of the ACLU for three decades.
> Engoron presided over a non-jury civil fraud trial related to
> real-estate valuations by the Trump Organization and stunned legal
> observers on both sides of the political aisle in February with a
> guilty verdict
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=71699&L=34163&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly9hcG5ld3MuY29tL2FydGljbGUvdHJ1bXAtY2l2aWwtZnJhdWQtdmVyZGljdC1lbmdvcm9uLTI0NDAyNDg2MWYwZGY4ODY1NDNjMTU3YzlmYzViM2U0P3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=85f8218aa4ee69f62ebf7bdb522238dc53f014e940f322a2c5f43698e3f724a7]
> ordering Trump to pay a staggering $335 million penalty—plus
> rapidly growing interest and additional fines. Trump immediately
> vowed an appeal and at a September hearing, New York appellate
> judges signaled skepticism
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=71699&L=34162&F=T&&hash=ee038e192371f6ca48eaac5d0f6a06d56e91038b8b61ff8c98dda6c9bc068c7e]
> about the Engoron ruling.
>
> _The Georgia Case Collapses_
>
> Meanwhile, in Georgia, Fulton County DA Fani Willis’s case against
> Trump for allegedly conspiring to change the outcome of the 2020
> election has collapsed. A state appeals court removed Willis and her
> entire office
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=71699&L=34164&F=T&&hash=a54df8834d943051fd652a23be7e3fb603abdf4df042d410ea9daa5aeeb2dda6]
> from the Trump prosecution over a conflict of interest involving a
> romantic relationship between Willis and another member of her team.
> The Georgia Court of Appeals panel said the “appearance of
> impropriety” was so powerful that “this is the rare case in
> which disqualification is mandated and no other remedy will suffice
> to restore public confidence in the integrity of these
> proceedings.” Willis, a longtime Democrat, can appeal to the
> Georgia Supreme Court, but the legal tides are running against her.
> Trump’s Georgia lawyer issued a statement saying that the decision
> “puts an end to a politically motivated persecution of the next
> President of the United States.”
>
> Judicial Watch has been investigating the lawfare against Trump for
> years. Our own Tom Fitton was dragged into a Jack Smith grand jury
> for, as he noted on X
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=71699&L=34165&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly94LmNvbS9Ub21GaXR0b24vc3RhdHVzLzE3MzQ1NzExNzY1NzAwODU1MDk/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=16524e87fdeba9d33240e11e4c88c0eab4aaaa5db77888efdcb20d3d81ac35bf],
“four hours
> of harassing questions about First Amendment-protected activity and
> debates about electors, tweets, what I ate for lunch at the White
> House, and whether I watched Trump’s election night speech. It was
> all about politics.”
>
> At Judicial Watch, we continue to closely track lawfare
> developments, push for more accountability, and report to the
> public. Among our recent moves, we’re seeking a special master
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=71699&L=34142&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvc3BlY2lhbC1tYXN0ZXItZmFuaS13aWxsaXMtbGF3c3VpdC8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=92eca92d5601482638ade33244983bdaa35e7f9f3263c7b67e93914506e9d15a]
> in our lawsuit for Fani Willis’s communication with lawfare
> warriors Jack Smith and the House January 6 Committee; earlier this
> month, Willis admitted communicating with the January 6 Committee,
> but released only one already public letter. In February, we
> protested
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=71699&L=31981&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvbmFtZXMtb2YtdG9wLXN0YWZmLWFudGktdHJ1bXAvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=f983bb930d170366a97627dca8c343257ec206b62fce008c7bee21f62e8ad904]
a
> Biden Administration move to keep secret the names of top Jack Smith
> staff. In 2023, we sued
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=71699&L=31611&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtZG9qLWZ1bHRvbi1jby1jb21wbGFpbnQtMDMxMTAvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=b8402c477e83198bea580e130f28cc68a2d972bd44e25c8c67f1161b695b4809]
> the Justice Department for records of funding and assistance between
> Smith’s office and Willis’s office, and we obtained information
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=71699&L=29822&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvYnJhZ2ctcGFpZC10by1zaHV0LWRvd24taG91c2UtanVkaWNpYXJ5LW92ZXJzaWdodC1pbnRvLWluZGljdG1lbnQtb2YtdHJ1bXAvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=84b544e6263a0f71f3f8f1b286d5c03d9bd00d835f6d312ede7bc8e320ffe224]
> showing Manhattan DA Bragg hiring high-priced lawyers to beat back
> Congressional inquiries into his Trump prosecutions.
>
> There’s more to come. Stay tuned.

HAPPY NEW YEAR!
_‘Get going. Move forward. Aim high.’_
This quote, attributed to President-elect Donald Trump, certainly
describes his activity since the election.

He has wasted no time in naming his cabinet and announcing his goals
for border control, health policy and environmental regulations. In
fact, given our current absentee White House occupant, Trump is now
our de-facto president.

As he wrote in his book, The Art of the Deal, “I like thinking big.
I always have. To me, it’s very simple: if you’re going to be
thinking anyway, you might as well think big.”

Here at Judicial Watch, we are equally resolute as we enter the new
year. Just this month we:

● Asked
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=71699&L=34142&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvc3BlY2lhbC1tYXN0ZXItZmFuaS13aWxsaXMtbGF3c3VpdC8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=92eca92d5601482638ade33244983bdaa35e7f9f3263c7b67e93914506e9d15a]
a
court to appoint a special master to oversee District Attorney Fani
Willis’ search for records in our lawsuit for communications she had
with Special Counsel Jack Smith and the House January 6 Committee.

● Sued
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=71699&L=34117&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvdHVsc2ktZ2FiYmFyZC10ZXJyb3Jpc3Qtd2F0Y2gtbGlzdC8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=46c7724f25a66264ae133477b9e675ad45ca7370ad5a536e366173f416f8259c]
the Department of Homeland Security for records on former Rep. Tulsi
Gabbard being targeted for surveillance under the Transportation
Security Administration terrorist watch program.

● Appeared
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=71699&L=34171&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvaHMtdGVhY2hlci1maXJlZC1wb3N0cy1jcml0aWNpemluZy1nZW9yZ2UtZmxveWQtcmlvdHMvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=b33b2b265b869085b3457525b346ea9d1a123ce3308f8427c79e2c29c5653b3c]
in court on behalf of Palatine, Illinois, tenured high school teacher
Jeanne Hedgepeth, who was fired after posting comments on Facebook
criticizing the riots, violence, and lootings in Chicago in the
aftermath of the May 25, 2020, killing of George Floyd.

These are a small portion of our growing portfolio of cases –
currently at 172 – aimed at ensuring clean elections, secure
borders, and transparency in government – as well as protection
against the Left’s attack on our constitutional republic, the
freedoms it protects, censorship of free speech, and abuse of power.
We appreciate your support in 2020.

4 as we have pursued these lawsuits and pledge to fight aggressively
on your behalf in 2025. As we close the year, I encourage you to renew
(or begin!) your support
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=71699&L=34172&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9uYXRlL3RoYW5rLXlvdS0yMjkvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=65c85387c14bf30d113a006d4c234180b81cc74b98a35160caa138769be56823]
of Judicial
Watch!

Happy New Year!

Until next week,

clean_body: Happy New Year! [INSIDE JW] Trump Wins the Lawfare War [] The Left hasn’t caught on yet that Donald Trump is a fighter who doesn’t fold easily. As our chief investigative reporter, Micah Morrison, reports [], Trump’s win at the polls helped lead to wins in the courts. Pundits and historians will be a long time sorting out the magnitude of Donald Trump’s electoral victory but one thing already is clear: Trump not only triumphed in the presidential contest, he also won the lawfare war. The latter—a victory for the constitutional foundation of the country —may prove as consequential as the former. “Lawfare” is a political war fought by other means: partisan warfare conducted in the courts and the media. Trump spent the entire Biden presidency battling lawfare cases brought by Democrat-allied prosecutors and judges—by Justice Department Special Counsel Jack Smith, Manhattan District Attorney Alvin Bragg, Georgia District Attorney Fani Willis, New York State Attorney General Letitia James, New York judges Juan Merchan and Arthur Engoron, and others. Trump fought back in the courts and in the court of public opinion. His election win not only deals death blows to the Democrat-aligned lawfare cases, but possibly to the practice of lawfare itself. Let’s take a moment to survey the legal landscape: Jack Smith Goes Down In November 2022, President Joe Biden’s attorney general, Merrick Garland, appointed prosecutor Jack Smith as special counsel for two Justice Department investigations: the January 6, 2021, events at the U.S. Capitol, and separately, alleged Trump mishandling of classified documents. It was a particularly brazen lawfare move because by that time, the outline of the 2024 presidential contest was clear: Donald Trump was the frontrunner for the GOP presidential nomination and Joe Biden was signaling that he would run for re-election. The Biden Justice Department investigating the GOP presidential candidate seemed an outlandish and illegal proposition [], but Garland and Smith pressed on. In July, Judge Aileen Cannon had seen enough and dismissed [] the classified documents case on the grounds that the special counsel was unlawfully appointed. In November, after the election, the Justice Department threw in the towel, moving to drop all January 6 charges against Trump on the grounds that a sitting president cannot be charged with a crime. Trump rightfully claimed victory. “I persevered, against all odds, and WON,” he wrote on Truth Social. He added, “These cases, like all of the other cases I have been forced to go through, are empty and lawless, and should never have been brought,” Bragg’s New York Criminal Case in Death Spiral Deep blue New York produced a cadre of lawfare warriors in pursuit of the once and future Republican president. One of its chief combatants was Manhattan DA Alvin Bragg [], who campaigned for office on an anti-Trump platform, reminding voters that he had “sued Trump more than a hundred times.” Before charging Trump in April 2023 with thirty-four felony counts of falsifying business records—generally a low-level misdemeanor—Bragg had led a civil lawsuit against the Trump Foundation and criminal cases against the Trump Organization and its chief financial officer. Trump was convicted in May on the business records charges, but his lawyers are asking that the case be thrown out on numerous grounds, including that any sentencing would unconstitutionally interfere with Trump’s conduct of a second term in the presidency. Bragg recently petitioned the court to put the case on ice for the entirety of Trump’s second presidential term—a move the Trump team ridiculed as “a total failure of the prosecution” signaling that the case is “effectively over [ Lawfare Judges Under Pressure Presiding over the flurry of appeals [] in the business-records case is Justice Juan Merchan, another New Yorker with a lawfare pedigree. Earlier this month, Merchan threw out [] Trump’s appeal to dismiss the case on the basis of presidential immunity. Like most New York judges, Merchan rose through the ranks of the Democratic Party’s political machine, which plays a significant role in state judicial appointments. Before becoming a judge, Merchan served as a prosecutor in the Manhattan DA’s office and worked for the New York attorney general. In 2006, Mayor Michael Bloomberg appointed him to a family court judgeship, and he was elevated to criminal court in 2009. In July, Merchan received a “caution letter [ from the New York Commission of Judicial Conduct warning him about donations to Joe Biden and other Democratic causes. Merchan’s daughter, Loren, is president of the left-wing digital advertising firm, Authentic Campaigns. Juan Merchan will have plenty of power over the Trump appeals in the coming months, but he will not have the final word. Trump can appeal to higher New York courts and, ultimately, the U.S. Supreme Court. Trump also faced a high-stakes legal assault from New York State Attorney General Letitia James in a civil fraud case presided over by Justice Arthur Engoron. James and Engoron both came up through the progressive ranks of the New York Democratic Party. Like Alvin Bragg, James used Trump as a punching bag in her campaign for political office. She denounced [] Trump as an “illegitimate president” and vowed to “shine a bright light into every corner of his real estate dealings.” Engoron, a longtime Democrat, protested the Vietnam War at Columbia University and has been a member of the ACLU for three decades. Engoron presided over a non-jury civil fraud trial related to real-estate valuations by the Trump Organization and stunned legal observers on both sides of the political aisle in February with a guilty verdict [] ordering Trump to pay a staggering $335 million penalty—plus rapidly growing interest and additional fines. Trump immediately vowed an appeal and at a September hearing, New York appellate judges signaled skepticism [] about the Engoron ruling. The Georgia Case Collapses Meanwhile, in Georgia, Fulton County DA Fani Willis’s case against Trump for allegedly conspiring to change the outcome of the 2020 election has collapsed. A state appeals court removed Willis and her entire office [] from the Trump prosecution over a conflict of interest involving a romantic relationship between Willis and another member of her team. The Georgia Court of Appeals panel said the “appearance of impropriety” was so powerful that “this is the rare case in which disqualification is mandated and no other remedy will suffice to restore public confidence in the integrity of these proceedings.” Willis, a longtime Democrat, can appeal to the Georgia Supreme Court, but the legal tides are running against her. Trump’s Georgia lawyer issued a statement saying that the decision “puts an end to a politically motivated persecution of the next President of the United States.” Judicial Watch has been investigating the lawfare against Trump for years. Our own Tom Fitton was dragged into a Jack Smith grand jury for, as he noted on X [], “four hours of harassing questions about First Amendment-protected activity and debates about electors, tweets, what I ate for lunch at the White House, and whether I watched Trump’s election night speech. It was all about politics.” At Judicial Watch, we continue to closely track lawfare developments, push for more accountability, and report to the public. Among our recent moves, we’re seeking a special master [] in our lawsuit for Fani Willis’s communication with lawfare warriors Jack Smith and the House January 6 Committee; earlier this month, Willis admitted communicating with the January 6 Committee, but released only one already public letter. In February, we protested [] a Biden Administration move to keep secret the names of top Jack Smith staff. In 2023, we sued [] the Justice Department for records of funding and assistance between Smith’s office and Willis’s office, and we obtained information [] showing Manhattan DA Bragg hiring high-priced lawyers to beat back Congressional inquiries into his Trump prosecutions. There’s more to come. Stay tuned. HAPPY NEW YEAR! ‘Get going. Move forward. Aim high.’ This quote, attributed to President-elect Donald Trump, certainly describes his activity since the election. He has wasted no time in naming his cabinet and announcing his goals for border control, health policy and environmental regulations. In fact, given our current absentee White House occupant, Trump is now our de-facto president. As he wrote in his book, The Art of the Deal, “I like thinking big. I always have. To me, it’s very simple: if you’re going to be thinking anyway, you might as well think big.” Here at Judicial Watch, we are equally resolute as we enter the new year. Just this month we: ● Asked [] a court to appoint a special master to oversee District Attorney Fani Willis’ search for records in our lawsuit for communications she had with Special Counsel Jack Smith and the House January 6 Committee. ● Sued [] the Department of Homeland Security for records on former Rep. Tulsi Gabbard being targeted for surveillance under the Transportation Security Administration terrorist watch program. ● Appeared [] in court on behalf of Palatine, Illinois, tenured high school teacher Jeanne Hedgepeth, who was fired after posting comments on Facebook criticizing the riots, violence, and lootings in Chicago in the aftermath of the May 25, 2020, killing of George Floyd. These are a small portion of our growing portfolio of cases – currently at 172 – aimed at ensuring clean elections, secure borders, and transparency in government – as well as protection against the Left’s attack on our constitutional republic, the freedoms it protects, censorship of free speech, and abuse of power. We appreciate your support in 2020. 4 as we have pursued these lawsuits and pledge to fight aggressively on your behalf in 2025. As we close the year, I encourage you to renew (or begin!) your support [] of Judicial Watch! Happy New Year! Until next week, [Contribute] [] [advertisement] [] [32x32x1] [] [32x32x2] [] [32x32x3] [] [32x32x3] [] Judicial Watch, Inc. 425 3rd St Sw Ste 800 Washington, DC 20024 202.646.5172 © 2017 - 2024, All Rights Reserved Manage Email Subscriptions [] |

clean_body: With a fully GOP-controlled government, we’re going to face challenges like never before. Peter — As we close out 2024, we’re looking ahead to what will be our next big test: The new year. With a fully GOP-controlled government, we’re going to face challenges like never before. But make no mistake – Kim and her colleagues in the House will never back down when it comes to fighting for everyday Americans. We know we’ll have to work tirelessly to hold this new administration accountable, and with your support, we will. But we need your help to ensure we have the strength to hit the ground running. That’s why our team has set an ambitious goal to reach $10,000 by the end of this month. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | = | | = | | = ---|---|---|---|--- = | | = | | = ---|---|---|---|--- Thank you for being a part of this fight, Team Schrier Rep.
